assertions.description: bus_topology.detected_bus_signals reports prefix='B', range='B1..B12', width=16. The range B1..B12 contains 12 possible indices; with 3 reported missing (B3, B7, B8), only 9 actual B-nets exist. A width of 16 is incorrect — it should be at most 12 (the range span) or 9 (the count of present nets). The value 16 appears to result from an off-by-one or power-of-2 rounding error in the width calculation. Note: the 'B' signals here are mostly unused passthrough pins on the Alveo U25 debug connector (U1), not a real parallel bus.

INCORRECT: J5 and J7 use reference prefix 'J' and are individually classified as category='connector' in the components array. However, because they share the same value (Conn_01x06) and footprint as JP1 and JP2, the BOM groups all four together and assigns type='jumper' (derived from the JP-prefix components). The BOM entry reads: refs=['J5','J7','JP1','JP2'], type='jumper'. J5 and J7 are connectors and should not inherit the jumper type from JP1/JP2 in BOM output. statistics.component_types correctly reports jumper=2 and connector=6, confirming the per-component classification is right but the BOM grouping type is wrong.

This design is explicitly a JTAG adapter for the Xilinx Alveo U25. All four canonical JTAG signals are present as named nets and connected: TCK (U1.A2, J1.Pin_6, R2), TDI (U1.A3, J1.Pin_10, R4), TDO (U1.A5, J1.Pin_8, R3), TMS (U1.A4, J1.Pin_4, R1). Despite this, bus_analysis only lists 'i2c', 'spi', 'uart', and 'can' — JTAG is absent entirely. This is a significant false negative given the design's primary function.
